name: Toasted Coconut Protein Cheesecake
description: Toasted-coconut variant of the vanilla base. Coconut runs throughout the filling (45 g toasted, fine-ground, beaten in) plus a 20 g toasted-coconut topping; crustless to stay lean-ish. Coconut is the only flavor source (the oil is neutral) so it is toasted hard for aroma. Macro note: coconut is ~64% fat, so this runs richer than the base - about 183 cal/slice, ratio ~12.3 vs the base 129/9.1. To-make; a tsp of coconut extract (0 cal) is the planned next lever to deepen flavor and drop the shredded amount.

payload.instructionText: Toast 65 g shredded coconut in a dry skillet over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until golden and fragrant (it colors fast then burns - watch it). Cool. Grind 45 g to a fine meal (spice grinder or food processor) and beat it into the filling with the wet ingredients in step 3 so the coconut disperses throughout; reserve the other 20 g for the topping.
Once the cheesecake is fully chilled, scatter the reserved 20 g toasted coconut evenly over the top just before serving (keeps it crisp).
